verses.text: Jehoram succeeds godly Jehoshaphat, and once secure on the throne, murders all his brothers. Power without grace turns deadly. The man who fears not God will trample even his own kin to guard his place.

Jehoram succeeds godly Jehoshaphat, and once secure on the throne, murders all his brothers. Power without grace turns deadly. The man who fears not God will trample even his own kin to guard his place.

Jehoram succeeds godly Jehoshaphat, and once secure on the throne, murders all his brothers. Power without grace turns deadly. The man who fears not God will trample even his own kin to guard his place.

Jehoram walks in the way of Ahab, having married his daughter, yet the LORD will not destroy David's house, 'because of the covenant' and the promised lamp to David's line. Grace spares for the promise's sake. God's faithfulness to His word outlasts the faithlessness of His people.

Jehoram walks in the way of Ahab, having married his daughter, yet the LORD will not destroy David's house, 'because of the covenant' and the promised lamp to David's line. Grace spares for the promise's sake. God's faithfulness to His word outlasts the faithlessness of His people.

Edom and Libnah revolt from his rule, and he builds high places, leading Judah into spiritual fornication. His kingdom crumbles as his idolatry spreads. The ruler who forsakes God soon loses both his people's loyalty and the land's peace.

A letter from Elijah foretells plague on his house and a lingering, fatal disease in his own bowels, because he walked not as his godly fathers but seduced Judah. The warning is exact and solemn. God marks the difference between a father's faith and a son's rebellion.

God stirs up the Philistines and Arabians, who plunder his palace and carry off his sons and wives, leaving only the youngest. The murderer of his brothers loses his own children. What we sow against others, God can let us reap in our own house.

God stirs up the Philistines and Arabians, who plunder his palace and carry off his sons and wives, leaving only the youngest. The murderer of his brothers loses his own children. What we sow against others, God can let us reap in our own house.

The LORD strikes Jehoram with an incurable bowel disease; after two years of agony he dies 'without being desired,' with no honouring fire and no place in the kings' tombs. A wasted life ends unmourned. To live without God is to die without love, however high one's throne.
